perturbation.original_text: For each Approved Account (as defined in section 4 below) received through Affiliate's site, Affiliate will earn a referral fee calculated in accordance with the schedule set forth below ("Commission") paid monthly.
perturbation.changed_text: For each Approved Account received through Affiliate's site, Affiliate will earn a referral fee paid monthly.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "(as defined in section 4 below)" and "calculated in accordance with the schedule set forth below" has been removed, introducing uncertainty in calculating the refferal fee. This creates an in-text contradiction if read within the context of the agreement which, later in section 19, gives Chase the right to change 'the amount of Commissions' without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. Without a clearly defined calculation schedule, affiliates are left to Chase's discretion with no baseline or process to refer to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly refers to a defined Commission schedule in section 4. The changed text removes this reference, creating uncertainty about how the commission is calculated. This directly contradicts the "Contradicted Text" which gives Chase the right to change the amount of Commissions without notice. Without a clearly defined initial schedule, the affiliate has no recourse to challenge Chase's changes, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Chase grants Affiliate a non-exclusive, nontransferable, revocable right to (a) access the Chase site through the links solely in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and (b) solely in connection with such links, to use Chase's logos, trade names, trademarks, and similar identifying material relating to Chase (collectively, the "Licensed Materials"), for the sole purpose of booking Chase products. Prior to using any of the Licensed Materials, Affiliate will submit to Chase for approval a draft of all proposed material that incorporates the Licensed Materials, together with a brief statement setting forth the proposed use of such materials and any other background or supporting material reasonably requested by Chase to allow Chase to make an informed judgment. All such materials shall be submitted to Chase at least seven (7) days prior to the date of first intended use. Chase will notify Affiliate of its approval or disapproval of such materials within five (5) business days of its receipt of all information required to be submitted. The approval or disapproval of such materials will be in Chase's sole discretion. Any materials not receiving Chase's specific written preliminary approval will be deemed disapproved. Affiliate may not alter, modify, or change the Licensed Materials in any way.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase grants Affiliate a non-exclusive, nontransferable, revocable right to (a) access the Chase site through the links solely in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and (b) solely in connection with such links, to use Chase's logos, trade names, trademarks, and similar identifying material relating to Chase (collectively, the "Licensed Materials"), for the sole purpose of booking Chase products. Affiliate may not alter, modify, or change the Licensed Materials in any way.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the process for approval of the Licensed Materials, the text introduces an in-text contradiction. The changed text doesn't specify how the Affiliate knows if they are allowed to use the Licensed Materials. This introduces uncertainty and creates a contradiction considering Chase reserves full ownership of the Marks and the Licensed Materials (as defined below) in section 14. Without an approval process, Section 14 is contradited due to the uncertainty around the material usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Chase trademarks, trade names and service marks (collectively, the "Marks") are the exclusive property of Chase. Notwithstanding anything set forth in this Agreement, Chase reserves full ownership of the Marks and the Licensed Materials (as defined below). All use of the Marks by Affiliate is limited solely to the use contemplated by this Agreement. All use of Chase Marks by Affiliate is subject to the prior written approval of Chase.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a clear process for Affiliate to gain Chase's approval before using licensed materials. The changed text removes this approval process entirely. This directly contradicts the section stating 'All use of Chase Marks by Affiliate is subject to the prior written approval of Chase' because it creates a scenario where the affiliate is granted the right to use the materials, but there is no longer a defined mechanism to obtain the required prior written approval.
